title: 12 FilterBoxBuilder: Tests, Config, Scripts, and References

steps.title: Overview: Tests and Tooling
steps.description: The output tour covered the user-visible artifacts. This final tour maps the support layer: Bun tests, Vite configuration, Boxes.py audit scripts, printer research notes, public assets, and the original Nukit reference folders.

steps.title: Project Tooling
steps.description: The project scripts make the local workflow explicit: dev server, typecheck, build, test, Boxes.py audit, upstream oracle, and preview server.

steps.title: Vite Host Configuration
steps.description: Vite is configured with allowed hosts for both dev and preview. This supports local and tunnel-based inspection without adding server-side app code.

steps.title: Boxes Port Tests
steps.description: The port tests check SVG generation, sheet dimensions, layer metadata, and operation labels. They protect the browser-side Boxes port against accidental output drift.

steps.title: Cut Panel Source Tests
steps.description: These tests assert that cut panels, not ad hoc SVG primitives, are the source for output and preview geometry. They also check assembled panel roles.

steps.title: Workbench State Tests
steps.description: Workbench state tests preserve URL compatibility. They check print-sheet preview decoding, fabrication method precedence, and canonical query parameters.

steps.title: Print Sheet Preview Tests
steps.description: Print-sheet preview tests target visual regressions that are easy to miss manually: z-fighting offsets, static STL orientation, and right-side-up plate assets.

steps.title: Upstream Oracle
steps.description: The oracle script runs the upstream Python generator and compares selected facts with the local TypeScript layout. It is a focused bridge for port validation.

steps.title: Boxes Port Audit
steps.description: The audit script compares generated summaries across ported Boxes generators. It helps identify which generators are parity-checked and which are still intentionally out of scope.

steps.title: Reference Material
steps.description: The repo also contains hardware and research context: printer bed notes, public assets, and original Nukit design folders. These files explain the physical constraints behind many defaults.

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.text: What is the role of the oracle and audit scripts?
answers: They compare the TypeScript port against upstream Boxes.py behavior
They upload builds to a production server
They replace the Bun test suite
steps.explanation: `scripts/airpurifier-oracle.ts` reads an upstream root at line 7, and `scripts/boxes-port-audit.ts` maps upstream generator files on lines 25-29.

steps.title: Summary
steps.description: The test and tooling layer checks browser geometry, printable splitting, URL state, visual preview assumptions, and upstream port drift. With the earlier tours, this gives a full map of where to read when changing a setting, a model, a preview, or an export.
